item.title: RBNZ Won’t Add Member to Policy Committee Ahead of Election
item.description: New Zealand’s central bank will not add a member to its Monetary Policy Committee ahead of the November election, leaving it with three internal and three external members.

item.title: India’s Textile Stocks Become Market Standouts on Trade Deals
item.description: Indian suppliers of T-shirts, bed linen and towels to global retailers such as Walmart Inc. are among this year’s biggest stock-market winners, with some investors betting the rally has further to run.

item.title: OpenAI Proposes Giving the US Government a 5% Stake, FT Says
item.description: OpenAI has begun discussions about giving the US government a 5% stake in the ChatGPT-developer, the Financial Times reported, citing two people familiar with the talks.

item.title: Asia Deals Top $750 Billion Despite Mayhem in Parts of the World
item.description: The world has faced plenty of disruption this year, but that hasn’t derailed business in and out of the Asia Pacific, where the volume of deals such as mergers and acquisitions has already topped $750 billion in 2026.

item.title: Foreign Traders Sell Biggest Pile of Japan Bonds in Three Years
item.description: Overseas investors offloaded the most Japanese bonds in three years in June, official data showed, after a lackluster performance left the debt trailing most of its global peers.

item.title: Warsh Must Beware of Curves Flattening to Deceive
item.description: The bond market is taking him seriously, but that’s not necessarily great news.

item.title: Business Jet Departures in Middle East Drop 30% During Iran War
item.description: Business jet departures in the Middle East declined about 30% in the aftermath of the Iran war, a trend that’s at risk of continuing until a peace deal is signed.

item.title: Japanese Bonds Fall as Inflation Concerns Curb Demand at Auction
item.description: Japanese bonds dropped after demand at a sale of benchmark 10-year notes fell to the lowest since April on growing fiscal concerns and worries the Bank of Japan isn’t raising rates quickly enough to contain inflation.

item.title: Iran’s Floating Oil Stockpile Swells as Major Buyers Stay Away
item.description: A hoard of Iranian oil is building up at sea, as the Islamic Republic struggles to find buyers before the expiry of a 60-day window granted by Washington.

item.title: Former Goldman Analyst’s AI Startup Is Said to Raise $22 Million
item.description: LinqAlpha Inc., an artificial intelligence investment research company, is close to raising $22 million in a Series A funding round, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Vietnam Central Bank Vows to Boost Growth Despite Price Pressure
item.description: Vietnam’s central bank will seek to balance support for one of Asia’s fastest-growing economies with containing price pressures that are expected to flare up through the end of 2026.

item.title: Sliding Yen, Robust Economy Give BOJ More Grounds for Early Hike
item.description: The Bank of Japan has an increasingly strong case to consider an early rate hike as business activity remains robust and the tumbling yen threatens to spur inflation above its price target.

item.title: CATL Says Mining, Not Refining, Is Battery Making’s Big Hurdle
item.description: Securing supplies of key raw materials has become a priority for the world’s largest manufacturer of electric vehicle batteries.

item.title: Iron Ore Advances as CMRG May Block Some Fortescue Deliveries
item.description: Iron ore advanced — with futures briefly topping $100 a ton — after China’s state-backed buyer of the commodity signaled that it planned to restrict some Fortescue Ltd. inventories held at mainland ports.

item.title: US Tech Rout Sours Mood For Japan, South Korean Chip Stocks
item.description: Japanese and South Korean semiconductor stocks tumbled Thursday after a selloff in US technology shares soured the mood across Asia. Meta Platforms Inc.’s plan to sell access to AI computing power also raised worries about oversupply.

item.title: India IPO Pipeline Hits Record as Big Guns Line Up
item.description: Bankers expect IPO activity to accelerate in the coming months.

item.title: Australia Runs Surprise Trade Deficit With Widest Gap Since 2015
item.description: Australia fell into a surprise trade deficit in May as exports dropped the most all year, with imports extending a streak of gains caused in part by high fuel prices and data center demand.

item.title: Japan’s Takaichi to Meet Modi on First Official India Visit
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi will hold talks with her Indian counterpart Narendra Modi in New Delhi on Thursday during her first official visit to the country, as the two sides seek to deepen strategic and economic ties.

item.title: Musk-Altman Judge Enlists Mediator to Resolve AI Titans’ Battle
item.description: The judge overseeing the legal battle between Elon Musk and Sam Altman and their dueling AI startups enlisted a mediator to resolve the dispute.

item.title: What Falling House Prices Means for Australians’ Mortgages and Wealth
item.description: Higher borrowing costs and weaker investor demand are dragging down home prices, with broader implications for the economy.

item.title: The Winners and Losers of Japan’s Higher Interest-Rate Era
item.description: The Bank of Japan raised interest rates in June to a level last seen in 1995, reflecting a growing conviction that Japan has moved on from decades of low inflation and stagnant wage growth.

item.title: Global Appetite for Korea Grows Ahead of 24-Hour Won Trading
item.description: Global investors’ interest in South Korea’s economy and capital markets has “clearly changed” for the better in recent years, the country’s top finance ministry official said, as the won prepares to shift to round-the-clock trading on July 6.

item.title: Data Center Firm Switch Seeks $2 Billion in Funding Round
item.description: Switch Inc. is kicking off a private funding round led by Andreessen Horowitz that could raise about $2 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: India M&A Funding Rule to Squeeze Private Credit, Moody’s Says
item.description: India’s decision allowing banks to fund acquisitions can potentially hurt private credit funds as it would squeeze returns in one of their most lucrative businesses, Moody’s Ratings said.

item.title: Mirae Says a Lead Underwriter Confirmed Its SpaceX IPO Orders
item.description: Mirae Asset Securities Co. said it carried out all its work related to SpaceX’s initial public offering in coordination with the lead underwriters on the deal, disputing a Bloomberg News report published June 30.

item.title: ShareChat, India’s Meta Rival, Plans $400 Million IPO Next Year
item.description: Mohalla Tech Pvt., which operates social network ShareChat, short-video platform Moj and subscription-based micro-drama app QuickTV, is seeking to raise as much as $400 million from a public market listing planned for next year.

item.title: Gold Extends Gain After Warsh Remarks Ease Rate-Hike Prospects
item.description: Gold extended a rebound after a speech by US Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh dampened speculation the central bank may hike interest rates this year to tackle inflation.

item.title: Korean Stocks Fall as Jitters Over Meta and Apple Hit Chipmakers
item.description: South Korean stocks slumped as Meta Platforms Inc.’s plan to sell computing power raised questions over excess in AI capacity, driving a selloff in chipmakers.

item.title: SEC Probes Alleged Insider Trades That Cost Susquehanna
item.description: The SEC is looking into Susquehanna International Group’s allegations that unknown insider traders made $100 million on options bets ahead of a recent Chinese regulatory crackdown on cross-border brokerages, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Emerging Asia Bonds Draw Global Funds Despite Fed Hike Fears
item.description: Foreign investors are piling back into Asian emerging-market bonds despite renewed Federal Reserve hawkishness, as expectations that regional central banks will keep interest rates elevated support the debt’s yield appeal.
